  ],
  "text": "The Node.js ESM (ECMAScript Modules) loader and CJS-ESM interoperability bridge is implemented across multiple JavaScript and C++ files in the nodejs/node repository (v22.13.0). The core architecture revolves around several key components:\n\n1. **Entry Point Detection** (lib/internal/modules/run_main.js): The `executeUserEntryPoint()` function determines whether to load an entry point as ESM or CommonJS using `shouldUseESMLoader()`, which checks for .mjs extensions, package.json type fields, and experimental flags. If ESM loading is required, `runEntryPointWithESMLoader()` initializes the ESM loader via the cascaded loader system.\n\n2. **Module Loader** (lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js): The `ModuleLoader` class is the central orchestrator for ESM loading. It maintains:\n   - A `#resolveCache` (ResolveCache from module_map.js) for caching resolution results\n   - A `loadCache` (LoadCache from module_map.js) for caching loaded modules\n   - `translators` for converting different module formats into ModuleWrap objects\n   - Methods including `resolve()`, `load()`, `import()`, `getModuleJobForImport()`, and `getModuleJobForRequireInImportedCJS()`\n\nThe `CustomizedModuleLoader` class wraps the base ModuleLoader to support custom loader hooks through a `HooksProxy` (from hooks.js). The `getOrInitializeCascadedLoader()` function provides a singleton instance of this loader.\n\n3. **Module Resolution** (lib/internal/modules/esm/resolve.js): The resolution pipeline uses `defaultResolve()` which orchestrates:\n   - `moduleResolve()` - Handles relative, absolute, and bare specifiers\n   - `packageResolve()` - Resolves package references\n   - `packageExportsResolve()` - Resolves package.json exports field\n   - `packageImportsResolve()` - Resolves package.json imports field\n   - `resolvePackageTarget()` and `resolvePackageTargetString()` - Target resolution logic\n   - `decorateErrorWithCommonJSHints()` - Provides helpful error messages for CommonJS/ESM mismatches\n\n4. **Module Loading and Translation** (lib/internal/modules/esm/load.js, get_format.js, translators.js):\n   - `defaultLoad()` and `defaultLoadSync()` fetch module source code\n   - `defaultGetFormat()` and `detectModuleFormat()` determine the module format (module, commonjs, json, wasm, builtin)\n   - The `translators` SafeMap in translators.js contains format-specific translators:\n     - 'module' - Standard ES modules\n     - 'commonjs' - CommonJS modules loaded via ESM\n     - 'json' - JSON files\n     - 'wasm' - WebAssembly modules\n     - 'builtin' - Built-in Node.js modules\n\n5. **Module Graph Linking and Evaluation** (lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js):\n   - `ModuleJob` class wraps a module and manages its dependency graph\n   - `_link()` - Resolves all module dependencies and builds the dependency graph\n   - `_instantiate()` - Instantiates the module and all dependencies synchronously\n   - `evaluate()` - Evaluates the module asynchronously\n   - `ModuleJobSync` - Handles synchronous require() of ESM modules in imported CJS\n\n6. **Native C++ ModuleWrap Binding** (src/module_wrap.h, src/module_wrap.cc):\n   - `ModuleWrap` class wraps V8 Module objects\n   - `Link()` - Links module dependencies using ResolveModuleCallback\n   - `Instantiate()` and `InstantiateSync()` - Instantiate the V8 module\n   - `Evaluate()` and `EvaluateSync()` - Evaluate module code\n   - `GetModuleRequests()` - Retrieves module's import statements\n   - `GetNamespace()` and `GetNamespaceSync()` - Gets module's namespace object\n   - `CreateRequiredModuleFacade()` - Creates a facade for require() in imported CommonJS\n   - `ResolveModuleCallback()` - V8 callback for resolving module specifiers during linking\n\n7. **CJS-ESM Interoperability** (lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js):\n   - `finalizeEsmResolution()` function (line 1258) bridges ESM resolution results into the CJS loader's expectations\n   - The CJS loader integrates ESM resolution for require() in imported ES modules\n   - Uses `createRequiredModuleFacade()` to create CommonJS-compatible wrappers for ES modules\n\n8. **Supporting Infrastructure**:\n   - `Hooks` and `HooksProxy` classes (hooks.js) - Implement the loader hooks API for custom resolvers/loaders\n   - `initializeImportMeta()` (initialize_import_meta.js) - Sets up import.meta object with url and resolve properties\n   - `ResolveCache` and `LoadCache` (module_map.js) - Manage caching of resolution and load results\n   - `compileSourceTextModule()` (utils.js) - Compiles source text into ModuleWrap objects\n   - `initializeESM()` and `initializeHooks()` (utils.js) - Initialize the ESM loader system\n\n9. **Dynamic Modules**: `createDynamicModule()` (create_dynamic_module.js) enables programmatic module creation for loader hooks.\n\n10. **Network Module Loading**: `fetchModule()` (fetch_module.js) handles loading modules from HTTP/HTTPS URLs for experimental network import support.\n\nThe linking pipeline flows from entry point detection through resolution and loading, creating ModuleJob instances that coordinate V8 Module instantiation and evaluation. The C++ ModuleWrap bindings provide the low-level interface to V8's module system, while the JavaScript layer manages the higher-level orchestration, caching, and interoperability with CommonJS."
}

conversation_events.text:
# Node.js CommonJS to ESM Module Loader Migration Surface

## Your Task

Find all JavaScript and C++ source files in nodejs/node that implement the ESM loader and CJS-ESM interop bridge. Identify: the ModuleLoader class (lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js), the CJS loader's ESM fallback (finalizeEsmResolution in lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js), the ESM translators for different formats, the ESM specifier resolver, the ModuleJob linking/evaluation pipeline, the entry point ESM detection (run_main.js), and the native ModuleWrap C++ binding.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/node--v22.13.0.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/node--v22.13.0` (nodejs/node)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find the ESM loader and CJS-ESM interop bridge files in the Node.js repository. Let me start by exploring the structure and locating the specific files and symbols mentioned.

I've successfully identified and analyzed the ESM loader and CJS-ESM interoperability components in the Node.js v22.13.0 repository. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Key Components Identified:

### Core Files (20 total):
1. **lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js** - Main ModuleLoader and CustomizedModuleLoader classes
2. **lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js** - ModuleJob, ModuleJobSync classes for dependency graph management
3. **lib/internal/modules/esm/resolve.js** - Resolution pipeline (defaultResolve, packageResolve, etc.)
4. **lib/internal/modules/esm/translators.js** - Format-specific module translators
5. **src/module_wrap.h/cc** - Native C++ binding for V8 modules
6. **lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js** - CJS-ESM bridge with finalizeEsmResolution
7. **lib/internal/modules/run_main.js** - Entry point detection logic

### Important Symbols (50+ identified):
- **ModuleLoader** class with methods: resolve(), load(), import(), getModuleJobForImport()
- **defaultResolve**, **moduleResolve**, **packageResolve**, **packageExportsResolve**
- **translators** SafeMap with format handlers (module, commonjs, json, wasm, builtin)
- **ModuleJob** with linking and evaluation pipeline
- **ModuleWrap** C++ class with Link(), Instantiate(), Evaluate() methods

### Processing Chain:
executeUserEntryPoint → shouldUseESMLoader → ModuleLoader.import → ModuleLoader.resolve → ModuleJob → ModuleWrap::link → ModuleWrap::instantiate → ModuleWrap::evaluate

The answer has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with complete file paths, symbol definitions, and a detailed narrative explaining how the ESM loader system integrates with CommonJS.

  "text": "The Node.js ESM (ECMAScript Modules) loader and CJS-ESM interoperability bridge is implemented across multiple JavaScript and C++ files in the nodejs/node repository (v22.13.0). The core architecture revolves around several key components:\n\n1. **Entry Point Detection** (lib/internal/modules/run_main.js): The `executeUserEntryPoint()` function determines whether to load an entry point as ESM or CommonJS using `shouldUseESMLoader()`, which checks for .mjs extensions, package.json type fields, and experimental flags. If ESM loading is required, `runEntryPointWithESMLoader()` initializes the ESM loader via the cascaded loader system.\n\n2. **Module Loader** (lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js): The `ModuleLoader` class is the central orchestrator for ESM loading. It maintains:\n   - A `#resolveCache` (ResolveCache from module_map.js) for caching resolution results\n   - A `loadCache` (LoadCache from module_map.js) for caching loaded modules\n   - `translators` for converting different module formats into ModuleWrap objects\n   - Methods including `resolve()`, `load()`, `import()`, `getModuleJobForImport()`, and `getModuleJobForRequireInImportedCJS()`\n\nThe `CustomizedModuleLoader` class wraps the base ModuleLoader to support custom loader hooks through a `HooksProxy` (from hooks.js). The `getOrInitializeCascadedLoader()` function provides a singleton instance of this loader.\n\n3. **Module Resolution** (lib/internal/modules/esm/resolve.js): The resolution pipeline uses `defaultResolve()` which orchestrates:\n   - `moduleResolve()` - Handles relative, absolute, and bare specifiers\n   - `packageResolve()` - Resolves package references\n   - `packageExportsResolve()` - Resolves package.json exports field\n   - `packageImportsResolve()` - Resolves package.json imports field\n   - `resolvePackageTarget()` and `resolvePackageTargetString()` - Target resolution logic\n   - `decorateErrorWithCommonJSHints()` - Provides helpful error messages for CommonJS/ESM mismatches\n\n4. **Module Loading and Translation** (lib/internal/modules/esm/load.js, get_format.js, translators.js):\n   - `defaultLoad()` and `defaultLoadSync()` fetch module source code\n   - `defaultGetFormat()` and `detectModuleFormat()` determine the module format (module, commonjs, json, wasm, builtin)\n   - The `translators` SafeMap in translators.js contains format-specific translators:\n     - 'module' - Standard ES modules\n     - 'commonjs' - CommonJS modules loaded via ESM\n     - 'json' - JSON files\n     - 'wasm' - WebAssembly modules\n     - 'builtin' - Built-in Node.js modules\n\n5. **Module Graph Linking and Evaluation** (lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js):\n   - `ModuleJob` class wraps a module and manages its dependency graph\n   - `_link()` - Resolves all module dependencies and builds the dependency graph\n   - `_instantiate()` - Instantiates the module and all dependencies synchronously\n   - `evaluate()` - Evaluates the module asynchronously\n   - `ModuleJobSync` - Handles synchronous require() of ESM modules in imported CJS\n\n6. **Native C++ ModuleWrap Binding** (src/module_wrap.h, src/module_wrap.cc):\n   - `ModuleWrap` class wraps V8 Module objects\n   - `Link()` - Links module dependencies using ResolveModuleCallback\n   - `Instantiate()` and `InstantiateSync()` - Instantiate the V8 module\n   - `Evaluate()` and `EvaluateSync()` - Evaluate module code\n   - `GetModuleRequests()` - Retrieves module's import statements\n   - `GetNamespace()` and `GetNamespaceSync()` - Gets module's namespace object\n   - `CreateRequiredModuleFacade()` - Creates a facade for require() in imported CommonJS\n   - `ResolveModuleCallback()` - V8 callback for resolving module specifiers during linking\n\n7. **CJS-ESM Interoperability** (lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js):\n   - `finalizeEsmResolution()` function (line 1258) bridges ESM resolution results into the CJS loader's expectations\n   - The CJS loader integrates ESM resolution for require() in imported ES modules\n   - Uses `createRequiredModuleFacade()` to create CommonJS-compatible wrappers for ES modules\n\n8. **Supporting Infrastructure**:\n   - `Hooks` and `HooksProxy` classes (hooks.js) - Implement the loader hooks API for custom resolvers/loaders\n   - `initializeImportMeta()` (initialize_import_meta.js) - Sets up import.meta object with url and resolve properties\n   - `ResolveCache` and `LoadCache` (module_map.js) - Manage caching of resolution and load results\n   - `compileSourceTextModule()` (utils.js) - Compiles source text into ModuleWrap objects\n   - `initializeESM()` and `initializeHooks()` (utils.js) - Initialize the ESM loader system\n\n9. **Dynamic Modules**: `createDynamicModule()` (create_dynamic_module.js) enables programmatic module creation for loader hooks.\n\n10. **Network Module Loading**: `fetchModule()` (fetch_module.js) handles loading modules from HTTP/HTTPS URLs for experimental network import support.\n\nThe linking pipeline flows from entry point detection through resolution and loading, creating ModuleJob instances that coordinate V8 Module instantiation and evaluation. The C++ ModuleWrap bindings provide the low-level interface to V8's module system, while the JavaScript layer manages the higher-level orchestration, caching, and interoperability with CommonJS."
}
